compiled_state.author: Claude

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PREREQUISITE REALIGNMENT â€” reflect recent codebase changes (2026-06). Same process applied to the plan-review epic 5fd2: inventoried the shipped foundations (grounding oracle 8f6c, workflow engine e6d9, prompt storage da27) against this epic's assumptions, and corrected the description (new '## Prerequisite foundations â€” current codebase state' section + AC edits). Closed prerequisite epics NOT reopened.

NOW CONCRETE (was 'to build'):
- Native review_code ENGINE exists â€” src/rebar/llm/code_review.py + MCP review_code + 'rebar review-code'; deterministic applies_to selection â†’ parallel reviewers â†’ aggregate_findings â†’ review_result. (Today SINGLE-PASS, not yet three-pass.)
- Provider-agnostic runner is the shipped PydanticAIRunner (src/rebar/llm/runner.py); LangGraph cut over (e6d9). 'Multi-provider runner' is now this concrete runner.
- The CODE reviewers (code_quality/security/tests) are front-matter contract-bearing prompts (da27): inputs/outputs contracts, execution_mode, dimension, applies_to globs (security.md â†’ auth/secret/security/signing). Derived index + CI drift gate + Inspect-AI eval seam back them.
- Grounding oracle (8f6c) ships an EXTENSIBLE detector registry â€” the substrate for Pass-2 code-grounding.

STILL THIS EPIC'S WORK (clarified, not new scope):
- THREE-PASS: review_code is single-pass today; Pass-2 verifier + Pass-3 deterministic gate are the OPEN framework epic 9da1 (already a blocker/depends_on) this migrates review_code onto.
- SECRETS/SECURITY auto-block: the oracle registry is the substrate, but only smell_generic + applicability detectors ship today â€” NO secrets/security detectors yet; this epic adds them (or wraps gitleaks/semgrep behind the detector envelope).
- SOURCE-SEPARATION: review_code is inline in llm/code_review.py + env-gated (REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM) but not isolated.
- GERRIT LLM-Review voter: no Gerrit integration in-repo (prerequisite epic d251, also a blocker).

NOTE: the related framework epic 9da1 ('Realign reviews to three-pass + migrate existing reviewers') ALSO has prerequisite overlap â€” da27 already migrated the 6 reviewers to the front-matter contract model â€” so 9da1's 'migrate existing reviewers' premise has partly shifted; flagging in case you want 9da1 realigned too.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
GATE VALIDATION (final_gate_3pass.py, v8 rubric, agentic Pass-2) on the realigned epic. RESULT: DET floor PASS (check-ac 6; clarity 7/5); 31 Pass-1 findings -> 0 BLOCK, 29 advisory, 2 dropped. Zero blocking (v1 posture).

The advisory count is high (29 vs ~10-13 on the mature plan-review epic) because b744 is a LEAN, UNDECOMPOSED capability epic (5.2k chars, 0 children) â€” the gate is flagging pervasive UNDER-SPECIFICATION (G5 'decompose'; T8 Gerrit LLM-Review integer-label vocabulary undefined; the T2/T4/T5x/T6/T7/T9/T12 overlay details). That is the expected 'this capability epic needs decomposition + detailing' backlog, NOT a defect in the prerequisite realignment, and remediating it = designing/decomposing the whole epic (a separate effort from 'reflect recent codebase changes').

FOUR findings that directly REFINED the realignment edits were fixed:
- E3/E6: AC1 source-separation must PRESERVE the existing review_code MCP tool + 'rebar review-code' CLI callers (re-export/shim).
- E1: AC2 three-pass migration must also RETIRE the single-pass evidence+aggregate path (no half-migration).
- T5b/COH (critical): AC5 â€” the hard secrets/security auto-block must NOT inherit the oracle's fail-open posture; on detector-input unavailability it FAILS CLOSED (or surfaces a coverage gap on the vote), so a high-precision block is never silently skipped.
- E1: AC3 reframed â€” the shipped PydanticAIRunner + review_code are PRECONDITIONS; the deliverable is wiring the capability onto them (the prior phrasing verified no new deliverable).

Re-verified: check-ac + clarity still pass. The remaining advisory backlog (decomposition + overlay/Gerrit-label detail) is the natural next step for this capability epic â€” flagged for a decomposition pass when the user is ready.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: DESIGN NOTE (from branch-ref brainstorm, REB-289 follow-up candidate): Option B â€” automatic ticketâ†’commit provenance. Deferred from the v1 of the 'code-reading ops take a ref arg + pin a SHA' design (v1 = option A: client supplies the ref, default origin/main tip, completion-verify resolvesâ†’pinsâ†’recordsâ†’signs the immutable SHA). B would have rebar DERIVE a ticket's actual merge/squash commit automatically â€” via commit trailers (e.g. 'Ticket: <id>') or a forge/PR query â€” so the signed completion_verdict pins THIS ticket's exact landing commit rather than a client-supplied or origin/main-tip SHA. Larger effort (new provenance subsystem; depends on a commit-message convention rebar would need to encourage/enforce). Revisit here once v1 lands.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PASS 1 review (T10 Infrastructure/IaC overlay): not-applicable. This plan is an application-architecture change (snapshot isolation for code-reading gates) and does not provision or configure infrastructure-as-code (no Terraform/CDK/Pulumi/Ansible/K8s/Helm, no cloud resource provisioning). Infra-adjacent concerns (AWS volume placement, EFS/NFS flock caveat, serverless lifecycle) are explicitly tracked out-of-scope on separate tickets (alto-fruit-punch). The snapshot cache + janitor is app-level filesystem management on an existing host, not IaC. No T10 findings.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS-1 REVIEW [T1 prior-art / novel-architecture justification] â€” applies to the linked "Repo-snapshot isolation for code-reading gates" plan.

VERDICT: CLEAN for T1 â€” no findings.

T1 bright-lines fire here (novel snapshot-materialization architecture, security/auth via HMAC-signed attestation + private-repo credentials, concurrency/scalability target). The plan engages the criterion well:

AFFIRMATIONS:
- T1(a) prior art considered: the Design paragraph names six relevant systems (GitLab Gitaly, Sourcegraph gitserver/zoekt, GitHub tarball API, Bazel, ccache, Nix, in-toto) and maps concrete design choices to them ("read content from the object store without a worktree; pin an immutable SHA; reaper + free-space cap ... match what those systems ship"). This is explicit, not hand-waved.
- T1(b) novelty justified / anti-repackaging: the plan deliberately does NOT claim novelty â€” it positions its core mechanics as matching established practice. The single deviation ("the spike corrected our janitor primitive â€” drop the PID lease; use kernel guarantees") is explicitly justified by a spike rather than asserted, which is the right anti-repackaging posture.
- T1(c) capability assertions: the one genuinely platform-risky assertion (EFS/NFS flock semantics) is surfaced and responsibly scoped out to alto-fruit-punch rather than silently assumed; the janitor/delete-on-last-close primitive is presented as spike-validated.

No T1 finding to raise â€” a well-specified plan that affirmatively satisfies the criterion passes.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS-1 REVIEW (G6 â€” approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection) on the repo-snapshot-isolation plan ("Repo-snapshot isolation for code-reading gates"). Grounded against src/rebar/signing.py, _commands/transition.py, llm/completion.py.

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria this chunk passes):
- G6 mechanism (snapshot path): the core mechanism â€” read content from the object store WITHOUT a worktree, pin an immutable SHA, atomic dir-rename population + single-flight + delete-on-last-close, reaper + free-space cap â€” is logically complete and its concurrency/atomicity story is sound and standard. The explicit rejection of `git archive` (lossy) in favor of `read-tree`+`checkout-index` (faithful) is a correct correctness call.
- G6 fitness-for-purpose: the attested/local split structurally bars the named footgun (local mode "NEVER sign"; "structurally barred from producing a signed verdict") â€” this directly solves the false-negative-on-wrong-branch incident, not a proxy.
- G6 alternative-selection: the chosen object-store-materialization approach is defensibly at-least-as-good vs. the obvious structural alternative (`git worktree add` per snapshot) on lock-contention/blast-radius â€” the plan's "no worktrees" choice is the better one and is grounded.
- G6 positive rationale: present â€” "grounded against mainstream practice (Gitaly, Sourcegraph gitserver/zoekt, GitHub tarball API, Bazel, ccache, Nix, in-toto)â€¦ match what those systems ship." Satisfies the positive-rationale requirement (no rejected-alternatives section required).

FINDINGS:

- [ ] [G6 â€” mechanism correctness, load-bearing seam] The plan asserts the attested mode will "record `verified_at_sha` on the ticket and SIGN over it. Reproducible" and AC2 requires a signed verdict to be "bound to `verified_at_sha`" â€” but the EXISTING signing mechanism deliberately does the opposite. `src/rebar/signing.py` signs only the canonical payload `{v, algorithm, ticket_id, manifest}` (`_canonical_payload`, lines 188-201) and records `head_sha` as AUDIT METADATA ONLY, by explicit design: "The signed payload deliberately does NOT include volatile git state, so a signature stays certifiable as the repo evolves (head_sha is recorded as audit metadata only)" (docstring lines 19-25). So as written, a SHA recorded on the ticket is NOT cryptographically bound to the signature â€” re-signing/tampering the SHA field would still verify `certified`. To actually BIND `verified_at_sha` into the signature (AC2's "bound to"), the SHA must enter the signed payload, which forces (a) a `PAYLOAD_VERSION` bump that INVALIDATES every prior signature, and (b) changes to `sign_manifest` / `compute_signature` / `verify_record` and the close-gate signing call site (`_commands/transition.py:396`, which today signs only `verified_manifest`). The plan does not name this integration point, does not acknowledge the design tension with the existing "exclude volatile git state" decision, and does not specify whether prior signatures are migrated/orphaned. This is the load-bearing reproducibility claim, so under-specifying it risks the implementer either (i) only recording an unbound SHA field (fails AC2's "bound to"/"reproducible") or (ii) silently invalidating all existing certified closures. Please specify the binding mechanism and the prior-signature consequence.
  - location: "## What" â†’ attested bullet ("record verified_at_sha on the ticket and SIGN over it. Reproducible"); Acceptance Criteria line 2 ("A signed verdict records verified_at_sha and is reproducible â€¦ bound to verified_at_sha"); src/rebar/signing.py:19-25,188-201; src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:393-396
  - suggested_fix: State explicitly HOW the SHA is bound. The lowest-blast-radius option that needs NO payload-version bump and NO prior-signature invalidation: thread `verified_at_sha` into the verified-step MANIFEST itself (e.g. a canonical step string `"verified_at_sha: <sha>"`), so it enters the already-signed `manifest` field via the existing `sign_manifest(ticket_id, manifest)` path unchanged â€” the SHA is then cryptographically bound and reproducible with zero change to the signing payload schema. If instead you want a first-class `verified_at_sha` payload field, the plan must own the `PAYLOAD_VERSION` bump + the "all prior signatures become non-certifiable" migration consequence and say so in scope.

- [ ] [G6 â€” alternative-selection, possibly-simpler missed path] Related to the above: the plan presents binding-the-SHA as if it requires a new signing surface, but a structurally-different, codebase-aligned alternative exists â€” bind the SHA through the EXISTING manifest channel rather than extending the signed payload. Since `sign_manifest`/`verify_record` already sign the full manifest and the close gate already produces `verified_manifest`, carrying the SHA as a manifest step is smaller blast-radius (no schema/version change, no prior-signature breakage) and equally reproducible. Coaching only: have the plan consider this manifest-channel binding before committing to a payload-schema change, and state which it chose and why.
  - location: "## What" attested bullet; src/rebar/signing.py:160-206 (parse_manifest / compute_signature already sign the manifest); src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:362-396 (verified_manifest is the existing signing input)
  - suggested_fix: (empty â€” defer to the planner's choice; the prior finding's suggested_fix sketches the manifest-channel option)

Everything else in the approach (snapshot materialization, concurrency model, portability, local/attested separation) is sound and well-specified for G6. The two findings both converge on a single seam: how exactly the pinned SHA becomes part of the SIGNED (not merely recorded) attestation.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PASS-1 REVIEW [T1 prior-art / novel-architecture justification] on "Repo-snapshot isolation for code-reading gates": CLEAN â€” no findings. T1 bright-lines fire (novel snapshot-materialization architecture; security/auth via HMAC-signed attestation + private-repo credentials; concurrency/scalability target), and the plan engages the criterion well. AFFIRMATIONS â€” (a) prior art considered: the Design paragraph names six relevant systems (GitLab Gitaly, Sourcegraph gitserver/zoekt, GitHub tarball API, Bazel, ccache, Nix, in-toto) and maps concrete choices to them ("read content from the object store without a worktree; pin an immutable SHA; reaper + free-space cap â€¦ match what those systems ship"); (b) novelty justified / anti-repackaging: the plan deliberately does NOT claim novelty â€” it positions its core mechanics as matching established practice, and the single deviation ("drop the PID lease; use kernel guarantees") is justified by a spike rather than asserted; (c) capability assertions: the one genuinely platform-risky assertion (EFS/NFS flock semantics) is surfaced and responsibly scoped out to alto-fruit-punch rather than silently assumed.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS-1 REVIEW (G6 â€” approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection) on the "Repo-snapshot isolation for code-reading gates" plan. Grounded against src/rebar/signing.py and src/rebar/_commands/transition.py. Confirms the prior G6 pass's central seam finding (re-verified live).

AFFIRMATIONS: snapshot materialization mechanism (object-store read, no worktree, read-tree+checkout-index over lossy git archive); concurrency/atomicity model (atomic dir-rename, single-flight, delete-on-last-close); attested/local structural separation that bars the named footgun; positive rationale grounded in mainstream practice â€” all sound and well-specified for G6. See structured findings JSON for the load-bearing signing-seam under-specification.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (T3 Integration feasibility â€” git CLI plumbing). Recording findings + affirmations.

AFFIRMATIONS (T3 criteria satisfied):
- Integration achievable as described: the plan integrates the git CLI via standard plumbing (`git fetch origin`, `rev-parse`-style ref resolution, `read-tree`, `checkout-index`) â€” all real, extant git subcommands. `git fetch origin` and `rev-parse` are already used in this codebase (src/rebar/_engine_support/reads.py, src/rebar/signing.py:309, src/rebar/llm/workflow/snapshot.py:84), so those are MATCH/already-used integrations.
- Auth/HTTPS precondition stated: AC "Private-repo fetch failures surface a descriptive, actionable credential error" names the auth precondition for `git fetch origin`.
- Portability preconditions stated: "pure-Python tar-free extraction, gettempdir/mkdtemp, flock+mkdir fallback, POSIX"; the EFS/NFS flock caveat is tracked on alto-fruit-punch.
- Spike machinery present: the plan notes a spike already corrected the janitor primitive (dropped PID lease for kernel guarantees), showing capability gaps get routed to a spike.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] Ground the "faithful (NOT lossy `git archive`) materialization via `read-tree` + `checkout-index`" claim against the specific content gaps it must close, or route the faithfulness comparison to a spike before committing. [criteria: T3] [location: plan "What" â€” "Materialization is faithful (read-tree + checkout-index, NOT lossy git archive)"]
  Evidence: The existing snapshot path (src/rebar/llm/workflow/snapshot.py) is the `git archive` implementation the plan replaces; its docstring (lines 23-27) documents PRECISELY what `git archive` drops: `.gitattributes export-ignore` paths, submodule contents (stops at the gitlink), and Git-LFS files (pointer text, not smudged content). The plan asserts the new `read-tree`+`checkout-index` primitive is "faithful" but does not name WHICH of those gaps it closes, nor verify `checkout-index` doesn't share them â€” `checkout-index` populates from the index and likewise does NOT recurse into submodule gitlinks, and whether it smudges LFS / applies export-ignore depends on the index population and configured filters. This is the load-bearing, asserted-but-unverified capability the whole "faithful" success criterion rests on (T3: asserted-but-unverified external capability = MAJOR). suggested_fix: empty (the correct faithfulness target â€” track export-ignore? submodules? LFS smudge? â€” is a design call I can't confidently fix from the plan text; either enumerate the gap closed and cite the git plumbing that closes it, or add a spike that diffs `git archive`-tree vs `read-tree`+`checkout-index`-tree on a repo exercising submodules/LFS/export-ignore).

- [ ] State the `read-tree` + `checkout-index` invocation's index/work-tree precondition that makes it "lock-free" and "without git-lock contention," since these plumbing commands normally operate on an index file (.git/index) that concurrent ops would contend on. [criteria: T3] [location: plan AC "Concurrent gates ... run without git-lock contention"; "What" â€” "lock-free materialization"]
  Evidence: AC-4 requires "no worktrees; atomic population" and the success criteria say "lock-free." `git read-tree`/`checkout-index` default to the repo's single .git/index, which IS a lock point (index.lock) â€” the lock-free property depends on a stated mechanism (e.g., a per-op GIT_INDEX_FILE in a tempdir, or `--prefix` checkout against an ephemeral index). The plan asserts lock-free materialization but does not name the index-isolation mechanism that delivers it; this is the integration detail that determines whether the concurrency AC is technically feasible as described. suggested_fix: empty.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PASS 1 review probe note (T8 overlay): verifying whether `verified_at_sha` binds into the signed payload vs. stays audit-only is load-bearing for the reproducibility AC. No action needed on this ticket â€” recorded for traceability of the cross-reference from the snapshot-isolation epic.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (T11 Data-migration / backfill safety [overlay]): SUBSTANTIALLY NOT-APPLICABLE, one narrow coaching item.

This plan adds a new persisted/signed field `verified_at_sha` (AC2; `## What` attested bullet), but rebar's persistence is an APPEND-ONLY event log reduced into ticket state (`append_event` â†’ `reduce_ticket`), and the signed-verdict schemas are additive with `additionalProperties: true` (src/rebar/schemas/sign_result.schema.json:7; plan_review_verdict.schema.json:51-58 already documents `material_fingerprint` invalidation). The plan proposes NO backfill, NO blocking DDL, NO large-table rewrite, and NO rewrite of historical records â€” the field is forward-only on newly written events. So T11(a) expand-contract, (b) batching, (c) resumability, (d) dual-write window, and (e) rollback/data-loss all PASS as not-applicable (there is no migration to execute), except for the additive change being trivially reversible (just stop emitting the field â€” no data loss).

ONE coaching item (not a blocker):
- [ ] State that `verified_at_sha` is an additive, forward-only field on the append-only signed-verdict record (no backfill of historical verdicts; pre-existing verdicts that lack the field remain valid/verifiable), and define re-verification semantics (a new attested run records a fresh `verified_at_sha`). Rationale: the plan never states the format-compatibility posture for records that PRE-DATE this change, so reviewers can't confirm no migration is needed. This follows the codebase's established additive-event + `material_fingerprint`-style invalidation pattern.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS-1 REVIEW (G6 â€” approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection) on "Repo-snapshot isolation for code-reading gates". Grounded live against src/rebar/signing.py:19-25,188-206 and src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:393-396. Confirms and re-affirms the prior G6 seam finding.

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 criteria passed): (1) snapshot materialization mechanism â€” object-store read, no worktree, atomic dir-rename population + single-flight + delete-on-last-close, read-tree+checkout-index over lossy git archive â€” logically complete and standard; (2) fitness-for-purpose â€” attested/local split structurally bars the named footgun (local "NEVER sign"), solving the actual false-negative incident, not a proxy; (3) alternative-selection â€” object-store materialization is defensibly at-least-as-good vs. `git worktree add` per snapshot on lock-contention/blast-radius; (4) positive rationale present (grounded in Gitaly/Sourcegraph/GitHub-tarball/Bazel/ccache/Nix/in-toto).

FINDINGS: both converge on ONE load-bearing seam â€” how the pinned SHA becomes part of the SIGNED (not merely recorded) attestation. The existing signer (signing.py:19-25,188-201) signs only `{v, algorithm, ticket_id, manifest}` and records git state as audit-metadata-only BY DESIGN; AC2's "bound to verified_at_sha" + "reproducible" contradicts that unless the plan names the binding mechanism. See structured JSON for the two checklist items + suggested manifest-channel fix (thread `verified_at_sha` as a verified-step into the existing `sign_manifest` manifest â€” zero payload-schema/version change, no prior-signature invalidation).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (T3 Integration feasibility â€” git CLI plumbing). Re-verified live against src/rebar/llm/workflow/snapshot.py + the fetch/rev-parse call sites; confirms and consolidates the prior T3 pass.

AFFIRMATIONS (T3 satisfied):
- Achievable as described: the plan integrates real git plumbing â€” `git fetch origin`, `rev-parse`-style ref resolution, `read-tree`, `checkout-index`. `git fetch origin` (src/rebar/_engine_support/reads.py:22, .github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml:102) and `git rev-parse ... ^{commit}` (snapshot.py:84) are already used in-repo â€” MATCH / already-used.
- Auth/HTTPS precondition stated: AC "Private-repo fetch failures surface a descriptive, actionable credential error" names the auth precondition for `git fetch origin`.
- Portability preconditions stated ("pure-Python tar-free extraction, gettempdir/mkdtemp, flock+mkdir fallback, POSIX"); EFS/NFS flock caveat scoped out to alto-fruit-punch.
- Spike-routing present: the janitor primitive was corrected by a spike (dropped PID lease for kernel guarantees) â€” capability gaps route to a spike.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] Ground the "faithful (NOT lossy `git archive`) materialization via `read-tree` + `checkout-index`" claim against the SPECIFIC content gaps it must close, or route the faithfulness comparison to a spike before committing. [criteria: T3] [location: plan "## What" â€” "Materialization is faithful (read-tree + checkout-index, NOT lossy git archive)"] Evidence: the existing `git archive` path it replaces (src/rebar/llm/workflow/snapshot.py:23-27) precisely documents what archive drops â€” `.gitattributes export-ignore` paths, submodule contents (stops at the gitlink), and Git-LFS files (pointer text, not smudged). The plan asserts the new primitive is "faithful" but does not name WHICH gaps it closes nor verify `checkout-index` doesn't SHARE them: `checkout-index` populates from the index and likewise does not recurse into submodule gitlinks, and whether it smudges LFS / honors export-ignore depends on index population + configured filters. This is the load-bearing capability the whole "faithful" success criterion rests on (T3: asserted-but-unverified external capability = MAJOR). suggested_fix: empty â€” either enumerate the gap(s) closed and cite the plumbing that closes them, or add a spike that diffs an `git archive` tree vs a `read-tree`+`checkout-index` tree on a repo exercising submodules/LFS/export-ignore.

- [ ] State the index/work-tree precondition that makes the `read-tree` + `checkout-index` invocation "lock-free" / free of "git-lock contention," since these plumbing commands default to the repo's single `.git/index` (an index.lock contention point). [criteria: T3] [location: plan AC "Concurrent gates ... run without git-lock contention"; "## What" â€” "lock-free materialization"] Evidence: AC-4 requires concurrent gates "without git-lock contention" and the success criteria say "lock-free," but `read-tree`/`checkout-index` operate on `.git/index` by default; the lock-free property depends on an unstated index-isolation mechanism (e.g. a per-op `GIT_INDEX_FILE` in a tempdir, or `--prefix` checkout against an ephemeral index). This integration detail determines whether the concurrency AC is technically feasible as described. suggested_fix: empty.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS-1 REVIEW (T8 â€” LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe) on the "Repo-snapshot isolation for code-reading gates" plan. The overlay applies: the affected operations are the LLM gates (completion-verifier, plan-review, ticket-review, code-review, spec-scan), each an agentic reviewer prompt with file tools + a typed output schema. Grounded against src/rebar/llm/reviewers/completion_verifier.md and src/rebar/schemas/completion_verdict.schema.json.

AFFIRMATIONS (T8 checks that PASS):
- Enum vocabulary defined: the new `source` enum has its full value vocabulary defined in-plan â€” exactly two values, attested (default) and local, each with its semantics spelled out ("## What"). No undefined-but-referenced enum.
- Decision rule co-located with the schema it governs: the sign/no-sign rule is bound to the enum where it is defined ("attested ... record verified_at_sha and SIGN over it"; "local ... NEVER sign ... structurally barred from producing a signed verdict"), and AC3 restates it. The rule that needs the enum sits with the enum.
- No ambiguous counter/state increment in the LLM layer.

FINDINGS (2): see the structured JSON. Both are proven instruction-locality / fallback gaps in the agent layer, not generic checklist items:

1. [T8(b)/(e) instruction-locality] The gate prompts today direct the agent to read "the repository" via read-only file tools (completion_verifier.md:23-25 "read-only access to the repository through your file tools"; the verify loop at :59-76 uses list_directory/search_files/read_file with no path root). The plan changes the read TARGET from the working tree to a materialized snapshot dir, but never specifies the locality protocol that points the agent's file tools at that snapshot dir per-run â€” nor whether the prompt is updated. As written an executor could leave the agent reading the server CWD (the exact footgun) while the operation believes it pinned a SHA. Specify how the snapshot dir is injected as the agent's read root (tool cwd / repo_root binding) and whether the gate prompts need an edit.

2. [T8(d) fallback for failed sub-step] The plan specifies a fallback for ONE failure (private-repo fetch -> descriptive credential error, AC6) but leaves UNspecified the verdict/behavior when attested materialization fails AFTER a successful fetch (read-tree/checkout-index error, extraction error, free-space cap hit mid-gate). Critically it does not state that a failed attested run must NOT silently fall back to `local` (re-introducing the unsigned-vs-signed footgun). Specify the fallback: attested materialization failure -> hard error / no verdict (fail-closed), never an implicit local read.

Note: the `verified_at_sha` binding-into-signed-payload concern is already covered by the G6 and T11 passes (signing-seam + additive-field posture); not re-raised under T8.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS-1 REVIEW (G6 â€” approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection) on the docs plan "Docs: private-repo credentials, descriptive errors, ref/source semantics, HMAC trust model" (the S6/docs companion to the repo-snapshot-isolation work). Grounded live against src/rebar/signing.py:1-56 and the repo-wide absence of any in-toto artifact.

SCOPE NOTE: this is a DOCUMENTATION deliverable (Scope: "Out: code (S1-S5)"). For G6 the "mechanism" is "author docs covering five named topics into the existing MCP-setup docs," which is a mechanically-sound, well-understood change with no real structural design choice â€” so much of G6 is PASS / not-applicable (no alternatives to manufacture for a forced doc-authoring solution). The one G6-relevant risk is mechanism/fitness CORRECTNESS of the FACTS the docs commit to describe.

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 criteria passed):
- Fitness-for-purpose: the plan solves the actual named problem (operators/agents need credential guidance + clear failures for the new fetch-from-origin requirement), not a proxy. The "Why" ties each doc topic to a concrete operational gap the snapshot model introduces.
- Mechanism correctness â€” HMAC trust model: the trust-model facts the plan commits to document are ACCURATE against the code. signing.py:1-33,49-56 confirms HMAC-SHA256, environment-bound symmetric key (REBAR_SIGNING_KEY / per-env .signing-key), key never leaves the environment, key_id fingerprinting â€” i.e. exactly "integrity + intra-domain authenticity only; no non-repudiation / public verifiability / transparency." Documenting that limit is correct and well-grounded.
- Mechanism correctness â€” ref/source + env knobs: the attested(default, pinned SHA, signs)/local(in-place, never signs)/origin-main-default semantics and the REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR + disk-cap + EFS/NFS-flock-caveat (xref alto-fruit-punch) all match the sibling plan + alto-fruit-punch's design note; documenting them is sound and the cross-ref is correct.
- Positive rationale: present â€” the "Why" states why these docs are needed ("the server must be able to fetch from origin ... Operators and agents need clear guidance and clear failures"). G6's positive-rationale requirement is satisfied; no rejected-alternatives section required.

FINDING (1):

- [ ] [G6 â€” mechanism/fitness correctness; documenting an artifact shape the code does NOT produce and the sibling plan has NOT resolved] The plan commits to "Document ... the in-toto Statement shape" (## What, HMAC bullet) and AC4 ("the in-toto Statement shape are documented"). But (a) there is NO in-toto Statement anywhere in the codebase â€” the actual signed payload is the rebar-native canonical JSON {v, algorithm, ticket_id, manifest} (signing.py:19-25,~188-201) with head_sha recorded as audit-metadata-ONLY, not an in-toto Statement/predicate envelope; a repo-wide search for "in-toto"/"Statement"/"predicate" finds no such artifact in signing. And (b) the snapshot-isolation plan's verified_at_sha binding â€” the very content an in-toto-style attestation Statement would carry â€” is an OPEN, UNRESOLVED design seam flagged by the prior G6/T8/T11 passes on this epic (does the SHA bind into the signed payload or stay audit-only? still undecided). So as written this doc plan risks documenting a format (in-toto Statement) that the implementation does not currently emit and whose final shape is still in dispute â€” i.e. the docs could describe an artifact that never ships, or ship describing the wrong envelope. Please ground/sequence this: either (i) document the ACTUAL signed-payload shape as it exists ({v, algorithm, ticket_id, manifest} + key_id + audit metadata), naming in-toto only as a conceptual analogy if intended, or (ii) make this AC explicitly DEPEND on the snapshot-isolation signing-seam decision (verified_at_sha binding) landing first, so the documented Statement shape matches what the code actually produces.
  - location: "## What" â†’ HMAC bullet ("the in-toto Statement shape"); Acceptance Criteria line 4 ("the in-toto Statement shape are documented"); src/rebar/signing.py:1-33,19-25 (actual payload is native, head_sha audit-only, no in-toto envelope); cross-ref the OPEN verified_at_sha binding seam (prior G6/T8/T11 passes on this epic).
  - suggested_fix: Reword the deliverable to "document the signed-attestation payload shape AS IMPLEMENTED in signing.py ({v, algorithm, ticket_id, manifest}, key_id fingerprint, head_sha/verified_at_sha audit fields), noting any in-toto Statement as an explicit conceptual analogy not a literal wire format," AND add a dependency/sequencing note that the verified_at_sha portion of the documented shape is settled by the snapshot-isolation signing-seam decision â€” so the docs are written against the resolved shape, not a speculative one.

Everything else in the docs plan's approach (credential requirement, descriptive-error doc, fail-closed-vs-local-still-runs, ref/source semantics, env knobs + flock xref) is sound, fact-accurate against the code, and well-specified for G6.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
REALIGNMENT + PLAN-REVIEW (2026-06, local build)

Realigned this epic from the superseded three-pass framing (9da1, OPEN at write time) onto the SHIPPED four-pass review KERNEL (3316/vivid-gang-day, closed): b744 is now a kernel CONSUMER (supply diff-assembler + finder/verify/coach prompts + code move-catalog; call verify_findings/pass3_over_findings/coach). code_review.py is confirmed a throwaway single-pass demo to RETIRE, not a migration target.

DEPENDENCY ANALYSIS (answering "what must be satisfied"): the kernel (3316), runner+v3 engine (e6d9), contract reviewer prompts (da27), and grounding oracle+detector registry (8f6c) are ALL shipped/closed. The only LIVE external blocker is d251 (AWS Gerrit provisioning) â€” and it gates ONLY the Gerrit voter + dogfood ACs, not the capability. The 9da1 blocker link is stale (superseded by 3316; both closed).

SPLIT DECISION: NOT split. The capability (passes 1-4 + overlays + deterministic verdict, exposed via the existing MCP/CLI surface) is buildable and testable on real diffs WITHOUT Gerrit. Rather than split, ACs are PARTITIONED into (A) Capability â€” unblocked now â€” and (B) Dogfood/Gerrit â€” gated on d251. Splitting remains optional if capability is to close independently of infra.

OVERLAY / agent-triggered escalation: the approved base->overlay escalation (Round-A glob triggers UNION base.recommend_overlays, deterministic overlay_union, one-hop, capped N=3, enum-constrained) is now a first-class AC. DERISKED with experiments on the real v3 engine (offline, no tokens): the agent's recommendation drove a second batch's membership; bogus ids dropped by the enum; the cap bounded fan-out; one-hop held. Kernel proven gate-agnostic with a code move-catalog. Conclusion: NO core workflow-engine change needed â€” only two trivial scripted ops (overlay_union, merge_findings) + consumer-seam plug-ins.

PLAN-REVIEW RESULT: rebar review-plan (local build, --source local) -> verdict PASS (no Layer-1 floor failures, no blocking findings). model=opus-4-8, verifier=sonnet-4-6, 19 LLM calls. 10 advisory + 9 overflow advisories (all minor/none). Valid advisories REMEDIATED in this revision: pinned cap N=3; chose v1 secrets/security path (wrap gitleaks+semgrep behind the oracle envelope); named the observable code_review_verdict artifact + sidecar; clarified the re-export shim contract (public surface preserved, implementation replaced); added a fail-closed RED-fixture test AC; added an implementation-sequencing section (WS1-6) addressing the "too many layers, no sequencing" finding.

CHILDREN: none currently. WS1-6 in the description are PROPOSED child stories (not yet created); WS1-5 unblocked today, WS6 waits on d251.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (T10 Infrastructure/IaC [overlay]): NOT-APPLICABLE â€” no findings.

The T10 overlay condition requires the plan to provision or configure infrastructure (cloud resources, IaC: Terraform/CloudFormation/CDK/Pulumi/Ansible, Kubernetes/Helm). This plan does not meet that condition.

**Rationale:**
- All new work in WS1â€“WS5 is purely application-layer Python (diff assembler, overlay catalog, scripted ops, gate YAML, detector wrappers, source-separation shim) â€” no IaC artifacts are authored or modified.
- WS6 (Gerrit voter) adds REST/event-wiring code that consumes an already-provisioned Gerrit instance; it does not provision infrastructure itself.
- All AWS infrastructure (EC2 instance ~8 GB / t4g.large, Gerrit + rebar MCP containerization, replication plugin, GitHub lock ruleset) is explicitly owned by the **separate prerequisite epic `d251`** (`alto-fruit-punch`), which this plan treats as an external blocker â€” not its own deliverable. The plan text states this cleanly: "`d251` (AWS Gerrit + rebar MCP provisioning) â€” required ONLY for the Gerrit voter + the end-to-end dogfood ACs, NOT for the capability."
- There is no Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, Pulumi, Ansible, Kubernetes, or Helm file in the plan's scope. The repo's `has_iac` grounding-oracle dimension is not triggered by any described file-impact.

**Affirmations (T10 criteria â€” not-applicable PASS):**
- T10(a) Remote state + locking: not applicable (no state to manage).
- T10(b) Least-privilege IAM: not applicable (no roles/policies authored here; IAM for the Gerrit/MCP instance is `d251`'s concern).
- T10(c) Idempotency & drift: not applicable (no IaC apply surface).
- T10(d) Blast radius & env isolation: not applicable (no destroy/replace risk in this plan's scope).
- T10(e) Secrets in IaC: not applicable (WS5's secrets/security detector work wraps gitleaks + semgrep behind the grounding-oracle envelope â€” that is application code, not IaC vars).
- T10(f) Cost & sizing: not applicable (no new cloud resources provisioned here).
- T10(g) Observability & ownership: not applicable (no new infra resources to instrument).

No T10 findings.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
DECOMPOSITION + CERTIFICATION (2026-06, local build)

Decomposed this epic into 6 child stories (deps captured as WS-aligned epic ACs), sequenced via depends_on:
- WS1 8e6f-3ad2-1f24-4b8b (low-cloud-wreck) â€” diff context-assembler + base reviewer (recommend_overlays). READY (no blockers).
- WS2 8e78-d515-3a98-4eba (make-rant-buoy) â€” overlay catalog + finder/verify/coach prompts + code move-catalog. dep WS1.
- WS3 8855-e5f9-05b4-4a04 (crow-knelt-fiber) â€” overlay_union + merge_findings ops + gates/code-review.yaml. dep WS2.
- WS4 98ff-d3b8-d2f7-4a86 (side-haiku-dew) â€” gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict + source-separation. dep WS3.
- WS5 bba6-69b9-b9d4-4ac0 (corn-pub-oat) â€” secrets/security detectors (fail-closed) behind the oracle envelope. dep WS3.
- WS6 4119-1a4f-19ff-4cfa (virid-trench-stone) â€” Gerrit LLM-Review voter + dogfood. dep WS4; gated on d251.
Each child carries file_impact + verify_commands. WS1-WS5 are unblocked today; WS6 awaits d251-05e1-eb55-43b2.

CERTIFICATION: ran the four-pass plan-review (local build, --source local) on the epic + all 6 children. ALL SEVEN VERDICT=PASS, zero blocking findings, no Layer-1 floor failures. Advisories were all minor/none (non-blocking). Store validate: no critical/major findings introduced; sibling depends_on links landed at story level (no mis-promotion).

REMEDIATION: applied the substantive advisories across epic + children â€” overlay-id enum ownership (WS1 owns the enum, WS2 the content); the 'CI drift gate' defined as a concrete observable (rebar workflow validate + prompt-index drift check); concrete threshold defaults (DEFAULT_BLOCK_THRESHOLD=0.95, advisory by default, blocking only for secrets/security); a registered typed code_review_verdict schema + REVIEW_RESULT sidecar; fail-closed = BLOCK (coverage-gap attached to the blocking vote); Gerrit label/auth/copyCondition/vote-mapping deferred to d251; a back-out plan for the destructive single-pass retirement (flag-off default + re-export shim). A post-remediation re-run (epic+WS2+WS4) re-confirmed PASS; residual advisories are non-blocking AC-phrasing nitpicks left intentionally (the gate is a structural floor, not a semantic score â€” over-fitting the finder would bloat the tickets without improving implementability).

comments.author: Iso Tester

PLAN-REVIEW REMEDIATION (Round-11, 2026-06-29) â€” disposition of the 20 advisory (+5 overflow, 3 dropped) findings. Derisk experiment evidence in session_log a7e2-381c-6dc6-4d0c (EXPERIMENT 1).

RESOLVED â€” gitleaks/semgrep fit (T1+T3 critical): PROVEN â€” semgrep is the opengrep YAML-rule format and passes metadata.rebar_envelope through verbatim + emits SARIF 2.1.0 â†’ ships as detector YAML on the existing opengrep backend (no new backend); gitleaks uses TOML + compiled-in rules (NOT a detector YAML) but emits SARIF 2.1.0 â†’ integrated via a generic SARIF-ingest backend (serves semgrep too). Pinned on epic + WS5 (bba6).
RESOLVED â€” fail-OPEN vs fail-CLOSED conflict (T3 critical): PROVEN Engine B is fail-open by invariant; fail-closed now lives in THIS gate's Pass-3 consumer (abstain on secrets/sec detector â†’ BLOCK + coverage-gap), not the oracle. WS5 AC.
RESOLVED â€” semgrep ruleset unspecified + diff vs repo scope (E2/T3 major): named vendored subset (p/owasp-top-ten + p/cwe-top-25), diff-scoped. WS5.
RESOLVED â€” WS2â†”WS5 threshold-resolver ownership (G4 major): WS2 owns threshold_for + ships the two security criterion keys as blocking_enabled=False placeholders; WS5 flips them True. Explicit on both.
RESOLVED â€” base-reviewer failure fallback (T8 major): empty findings + glob-only Round-B + coverage-gap; never auto-BLOCK (recall-side). WS1.
RESOLVED â€” recommend_overlays output schema fields (T8 major): {overlay_id enum, reason required â‰¤200 chars}; dual named outputs. WS1.
RESOLVED â€” overlay_union already-run input wiring (T8 major): explicit with: input from the Round-A triggers output. WS3.
RESOLVED â€” merge_findings dedup/clustering (G6 major): clusters by file-anchor+dimension+line-proximity before Pass-2. WS3.
RESOLVED â€” escalation-mechanism ADR (T7/T5e major): ADR for the one-hop baseâ†’overlay design. WS3.
RESOLVED â€” gates/code-review.yaml schema doc (T7 major): documented schema AC. WS3.
RESOLVED â€” back-out shim return value + one-way-door contradiction (T4 + COH/E1 major): disabled â†’ review_code() returns valid empty review_result (+ disabled note), never error/stub; reverting the flag yields INERT, NOT restored single-pass â€” stated honestly; ADR records the one-way door. WS4 + epic.
RESOLVED â€” Gerrit voter staged rollout (T12 critical): per-project/staged opt-in before fleet-wide. WS6 + epic.
RESOLVED â€” fail-closed BLOCK surfacing in Gerrit vote (G4 minor): coverage-gap -1 surfaced distinctly from a real-finding -1. WS6.
RESOLVED â€” stale code_review.py references (T7 minor): WS4 audits docs/CLI-help.
RESOLVED â€” diff-assembler import survives WS4 retirement (G4 major): assemble.py has no code_review.py import; test pins it. WS1.
NOTED â€” G5 (first e2e vertical slice only at WS4): kept. The epic already documents WS1-WS5 are fully testable via the rebar review-code CLI/MCP without Gerrit, and WS6 is de-riskable against a STUB Gerrit; a thinner slice would re-cut the WS boundaries for marginal gain. Justified, not silently ignored.
NOTED â€” P6 (DoD quality on child ACs): addressed by the per-child AC additions above.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
IMPLEMENTATION KICKOFF + plan-review advisory triage (2026-06-30, worktree code-review-capability, local build).

Earned a fresh certified plan-review attestation (verdict PASS, 0 blocking, 20 advisory; model=opus-4-8 verifier=sonnet-4-6; signed key_id 412fa5372ad877e0 over head 66f0220d7) and claimed the epic to begin implementing WS1-WS5 (the capability; WS6 stays gated on d251).

Advisory disposition â€” the epic is a container; nearly all 20 advisories target CHILD-STORY implementation detail and will be genuinely resolved + re-plan-reviewed when each child is claimed/implemented:
- Overlay enum vocabulary not enumerated (A6,A17) -> WS1 defines OVERLAY_IDS + base prompt enumerates them.
- Overlay batch-step failure fallback unspecified (A11) -> resolve in WS3 (failed overlay -> empty findings + coverage-gap note, never auto-BLOCK; recall-side).
- WS5 abstain->BLOCK bridge two-option fork left open (A2,A3) -> pick + test ONE concrete path in WS5.
- WS2<->WS5 shared criterion-key names not pinned (A7) -> pin exact key names as a cross-story contract in WS2, WS5 flips those same keys.
- gitleaks/semgrep SARIF backend = NEW Engine-B code vs epic 'no core-engine change' claim (A1,A4,A12) -> the 'no change' claim is about the WORKFLOW engine; Engine B gains a SARIF-ingest backend. Reconcile wording in WS5.
- semgrep ruleset 'subset' + licensing underspecified (A5,A8) -> WS5 names a concrete rule-selection criterion + vendoring/licensing posture.
- Fail-closed split deserves its own ADR (A10) -> add ADR in WS5 (separate from WS4's single-pass-retirement ADR).
- WS6 adapter ctx-constructibility + rollback test (A9,A18) -> WS6 (gated on d251).
- Sequencing: kernel integration only validated at WS3/WS4 (A16) -> accepted; the WS boundaries are the contract seams.
- Cap N=3 calibration (A20) -> already documented as deferred.
EPIC-TEXT items to verify/fix during WS5: A14/A15 (epic says 'semgrep passes metadata.rebar_envelope through verbatim' but engine_b.py:302-309 says semgrep does NOT carry custom metadata into SARIF and rebar RE-INJECTS it into rule.properties.rebar_envelope â€” verify against code, correct the pinned-decision wording) and A19 (verbatim duplicate paragraphs to dedupe).

compiled_state.description:
# Agentic code-review capability for rebar (off by default, source-separated), dogfooded via the Gerrit LLM-Review gate

## âš‘ CURRENT STATE â€” 2026-07-01 (handoff; see session_log `risky-cherry-seal`/b009)
**MERGED to `main`** (PR #102, commit `2d3e7611`): the full four-pass code-review capability
(WS1â€“WS5) + the Gerrit review-bot rewired to it (WS6). The capability is off-by-default + source-
separated; Gerrit's `LLM-Review` vote now runs the four-pass review via `review_bot/adapter.py`.

**Children:** WS1â€“WS5 CLOSED Â· WS6 (4119) CLOSED Â· **WS8 (f2d6) OPEN** Â· **WS7 (1bd2) OPEN** Â·
**WS6-b (0da5) OPEN**.

**Remaining work is LIVE-INFRA and must run IN THIS ORDER â€” each is gated on access this session
lacked (navapbc GitHub org-admin + working AWS/SSM to the Gerrit host):**
1. **WS8 â€” Harden Gerrit auth FIRST.** The live Gerrit runs `auth.type =
   DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT` (dev-mode; anyone can impersonate anyone). Confirmed strategy:
   **GitHub OAuth via `gerrit-oauth-provider`**. Full procedure in
   `infra/runbooks/gerrit-auth-hardening.md` (PR #104). DO NOT skip â€” cutting `main` over to a
   dev-auth Gerrit is a security downgrade.
2. **WS7 â€” Make Gerrit the only path into GitHub `main`** (cutover; consumes d251/S6 mirror-lock)
   + write repo-root `CONTRIBUTING.md` (the Gerrit contribution flow incl. how new devs provision
   credentials) + a tested rollback. Only after WS8 verifies.
3. **WS6-b â€” Live dogfood:** land `CONTRIBUTING.md` THROUGH Gerrit (`refs/for/main` â†’ LLM-Review
   vote â†’ submit â†’ replicate), recording the change URL + vote.

The epic CLOSES only when WS8â†’WS7â†’WS6-b complete. Everything below is the original plan (for
context); the workstream list + ACs at the bottom are current.

---


## Context
Build the agentic code-review capability as a SEPARATE, OFF-BY-DEFAULT rebar capability whose source is clearly isolated so it has no effect when disabled. It CONSUMES the shared **four-pass review kernel** (evidence -> verify -> decide -> coach) and is enforced through Gerrit's `LLM-Review` label on the AWS-hosted environment. Dogfooding on rebar itself is the success criterion.

## Realignment (2026-06) â€” this epic now CONSUMES a shipped kernel
This epic was written against a "three-pass framework being built in epic `9da1` (OPEN)". That is stale on two counts, corrected here (closed prerequisite epics are NOT reopened):

- **The framework grew a 4th pass and SHIPPED.** `9da1` ("three-pass; migrate existing reviewers") was SUPERSEDED by `3316`/vivid-gang-day â€” the **four-pass review KERNEL** (Pass-1 finder evidence; Pass-2 separate binary verifier; Pass-3 deterministic decide; Pass-4 affirmative coach) â€” which is **CLOSED** and shipped as `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/` (`verify.py` / `decide.py` / `coach.py`). `3316` explicitly `blocks` this epic and `docs/review-kernel.md` names b744 as "the second consumer". So b744 is no longer a from-scratch passes build â€” it is a **kernel CONSUMER**: supply the per-gate plug-ins and call `verify_findings` / `pass3_over_findings` / `coach` directly.
- **`code_review.py` is not a migration target â€” it is a throwaway single-pass demo to RETIRE.** Today it does select-by-glob -> parallel reviewers -> `aggregate_findings` -> `finalize_findings`, with NO Pass-2/3/4. The deliverable is the kernel-consuming gate; the single-pass `aggregate_findings`-only route is removed (no half-migration).

## Current substrate (shipped PRECONDITIONS vs this epic's NEW work)
SHIPPED (build on; do not rebuild):
- The **four-pass kernel** `review_kernel` (`3316`) + its documented consumer seam (`docs/review-kernel.md`): Pass-2 `verify_findings(findings, *, context, run_chunk, window_tokens, est_tokens, headroom)` + the single `verification` contract; Pass-3 `pass3_decide` / `pass3_over_findings(..., threshold_for)`; Pass-4 `coach(surviving, registry, *, pick, active_triggers)` + `MOVE_REGISTRY_SCHEMA` / `validate_move_registry` (with the `applies_when` field) + `VERIFIER_RULES_SCAFFOLD`. Verified gate-agnostic (consumes a code move-catalog + code findings directly).
- The **provider-agnostic `PydanticAIRunner`** (`e6d9`) + the **v3 workflow engine** (steps/branch/batch/map/loop, `RunnerAgentStep`, `ProductionBatchRunner`, `gate_dispatch`).
- **Contract-bearing code reviewer prompts** (`da27`): `code_quality` / `security` / `tests` front-matter prompts with `applies_to` globs + a derived index + CI drift gate.
- The **grounding oracle + detector registry** (`8f6c`): the fail-open evidence oracle + an extensible detector registry (built-in + project `.rebar/detectors/`).

NEW work (this epic):
- The four-pass code-review GATE that consumes the kernel (diff context-assembler, finder/verify/coach prompts, code move-catalog, per-criterion thresholds) + the thin `gates/code-review.yaml` + `gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict`.
- The OVERLAY catalog (the ~11 specialist reviewers drafted in the catalog comments) + the base->overlay ESCALATION mechanism (below).
- Secrets / High-Critical-security DETECTORS behind the grounding oracle's detector envelope (or wrap gitleaks/semgrep), with FAIL-CLOSED posture for the hard auto-block.
- SOURCE-SEPARATION into an optional module preserving the MCP `review_code` tool + `rebar review-code` CLI via re-export.
- Rewire d251's SHIPPED `review_bot` adapter seam to the four-pass gate (d251's S4b shipped the receiver/voter/client + the single-pass `adapter.code_review_decision`; WS6 swaps only its decision engine), and make Gerrit the enforced GitHub gatekeeper (WS7).

## Design â€” four-pass consumer + base->overlay escalation (mechanism PROVEN on the v3 engine)
Workflow shape (the same shape plan-review runs, plus a second agent-driven finder round):

```
diff
 -> assemble_diff (uses)                  # diff + changed_files context        [NEW diff assembler]
 -> triggers      (uses: overlay_triggers)# Round-A glob/keyword overlay flags   [EXISTING op]
 -> base          (agent, standalone)     # base reviewer: findings + recommend_overlays
 -> round_a       (batch, when:=triggers) # deterministically-triggered overlays
 -> union         (uses: overlay_union)   # (glob U base.recommend) - already-run, capped, ONE-HOP  [NEW ~30-LOC op]
 -> round_b       (batch, when:=union)    # the ESCALATED overlays (agent-triggered)
 -> merge         (uses)                  # base + round_a + round_b findings
 -> verify (Pass-2) -> decide (Pass-3) -> coach (Pass-4)   # all kernel
```

Overlay activation = UNION of (a) overlays whose deterministic triggers (`applies_to` globs / content signatures) match the diff, and (b) overlays the BASE reviewer escalates to via a bounded `recommend_overlays: [{overlay_id, reason}]` signal, `overlay_id` constrained to the FIXED catalog enum. A DETERMINISTIC orchestrator takes the union (minus already-run), caps it (top-N), dispatches Round B, and ALL findings (base + Round-A + Round-B) flow into the SAME Pass-2 verifier + Pass-3 deterministic gate. Bounds: ONE HOP (overlays do not re-escalate); per-change CAP; escalation lives on the recall/selection side only (a false escalation costs one extra advisory pass, never a wrong verdict; the precision/decision side stays deterministic). The base reviewer is a STANDALONE agent step (not a batch criterion) so its `recommend_overlays` output survives (the batch runner aggregates only `findings`).

Engine fit: requires NO core-engine change. `batch` membership is a static authored list (the fixed overlay enum) gated per-item by `when: ${{ steps.union.outputs.include_<overlay> }}` â€” exactly how plan-review's finder batch toggles criteria. A `uses -> batch -> uses -> batch` chain is expressible. The only new engine-side code is two trivial deterministic scripted ops (`overlay_union`, `merge_findings`); the mechanism was proven end-to-end OFFLINE on the v3 engine (the agent's recommendation drove Round-B membership; bogus ids dropped by the enum; the cap bounded fan-out; one-hop held).

## Dependencies â€” what must be satisfied to implement successfully
- SATISFIED: four-pass kernel (`3316`, closed) + consumer seam; runner + v3 engine (`e6d9`, closed); contract reviewer prompts (`da27`, closed); grounding oracle + detector registry (`8f6c`, closed).
- INTERNAL NEW WORK (in this epic; no external blocker): secrets / High-Critical-security detectors (fail-closed) on the `8f6c` registry; the diff assembler; the overlay catalog + prompts; the two scripted ops; source separation.
- EXTERNAL, NOW CLOSED + MERGED: **`d251` (AWS Gerrit + rebar MCP provisioning)** â€” shipped `src/rebar/review_bot/` (receiver `app.py` + `voter.py` + `gerrit_client.py` + the `adapter.code_review_decision(diff_text, repo_root, ref)` seam) AND the GitHub mirror-lock tooling (S6). WS6 now REWIRES that shipped adapter to the four-pass gate (an offline swap, no live env); WS7 executes the GitHub cutover. `1fa8` (CI second "Verified" vote) is `relates_to`, out of scope for v1.
- PROVENANCE: `9da1` (superseded by `3316`), `3316`, and `d251` are ALL now closed/merged â€” there is NO remaining OPEN blocker. The only gated items are the LIVE steps (WS6-b dogfood, WS7 apply), gated on deployed-env / GitHub-admin access, not on an open ticket.

## Why this epic is NOT split (and the alternative)
The code-review CAPABILITY (passes 1-4 + overlays + the deterministic verdict, exposed via the existing MCP `review_code` tool + `rebar review-code` CLI) delivers value and is FULLY TESTABLE on real diffs WITHOUT Gerrit â€” so it does not need `d251`. Bundling it with the Gerrit dogfood couples a software deliverable to AWS infra on a different timeline. Rather than split the epic, the ACs below are PARTITIONED into (A) Capability â€” buildable and verifiable NOW against the shipped kernel â€” and (B) Dogfood/Gerrit â€” gated on `d251`. This keeps one epic with one provenance trail while making the dependency boundary explicit; splitting remains an option only if the capability is to be released/closed independently of the infra.

## Decisions pinned during plan-review (2026-06)
These resolve advisory findings the four-pass plan-review gate raised against this plan (verdict PASS):
- **Overlay cap N = 3** to start (conservative; aligns with the orchestrator-worker "3-5 subagents" prior art). Calibration of N + the advisory/block thresholds is DEFERRED (shared stance with the kernel epic â€” thresholds start high, so v1 is mostly advisory); the cap/threshold values live in `gates/code-review.yaml` config, not hard-coded.
- **Secrets/security detector path (v1 default):** WRAP existing scanners behind the grounding-oracle detector envelope â€” **gitleaks** for secrets, **semgrep** for High-Critical security â€” as the deterministic ground-truth pre-pass (the LLM triages/prioritizes, never invents). Native first-party detectors are a follow-on, not v1. (Alternative weighed: author native detectors first â€” rejected for v1 because the scanners already encode high-precision rule sets; scanner-as-ground-truth is settled production practice.) **Integration shape pinned by derisk experiment (Round-11; session_log a7e2-381c-6dc6-4d0c):** semgrep IS the opengrep rule format (passes `metadata.rebar_envelope` through + emits SARIF 2.1.0) â†’ High-Critical security ships as detector YAML on the existing `opengrep` backend (vendored pinned p/owasp-top-ten + p/cwe-top-25 subset, diff-scoped). gitleaks uses TOML + compiled-in rules (no YAML rule file) so it can't be a detector YAML, but emits SARIF 2.1.0 â†’ integrated via a generic SARIF-ingest backend (`sarif.from_sarif(trust_rebar_bag=False)`) that also serves semgrep. Fail-CLOSED lives in THIS gate's Pass-3 consumer (abstain on a secrets/High-Critical detector â†’ BLOCK + coverage-gap), NOT in Engine B (fail-open invariant, must stay so). Detail on WS5 (bba6).
  - **Integration shape pinned by derisk experiment (Round-11; session_log a7e2-381c-6dc6-4d0c):** semgrep IS the opengrep rule format and passes `metadata.rebar_envelope` through verbatim + emits SARIF 2.1.0 â†’ High-Critical security ships as **detector YAML on the existing `opengrep` backend** (vendored pinned `p/owasp-top-ten`+`p/cwe-top-25` subset, diff-scoped). gitleaks uses TOML + compiled-in rules (no YAML rule file) so it CANNOT be a detector YAML, but it emits SARIF 2.1.0 â†’ integrated via a **generic SARIF-ingest backend** (`sarif.from_sarif(trust_rebar_bag=False)`), which also serves semgrep and any future SARIF tool. **Fail-CLOSED lives in THIS gate's Pass-3 consumer** (an `abstain` on a secrets/High-Critical detector â†’ BLOCK + coverage-gap note), NOT in Engine B, whose cardinal invariant is fail-OPEN and must stay so for other consumers. Details on WS5 (bba6).
- **Observable verdict artifact:** the gate returns a typed `code_review_verdict` dict (`{verdict: PASS|BLOCK, blocking[], advisory[], coaching[], coverage}`) + emits a review sidecar â€” exactly parallel to plan-review's verdict + sidecar, so the verdict is a checkable artifact (not just a Gerrit side effect).
- **Re-export shim contract:** the PUBLIC SURFACE is preserved unchanged â€” the `rebar.llm.review_code(...)` callable, the MCP `review_code` tool, and the `rebar review-code` CLI keep their names/signatures and continue to return a `review_result`. What changes is the IMPLEMENTATION behind that surface: the four-pass kernel-consuming gate REPLACES the single-pass `evidence + aggregate_findings`-only route, which becomes unreachable. `aggregate_findings`/`normalize_finding` may survive as internal Pass-1 helpers; the single-pass GATE PATH does not. The destructive removal of the demo path is recorded in an ADR (one-way door).

## Implementation sequencing (workstreams) â€” now child stories
The capability is decomposed into child stories (created 2026-06), sequenced via `depends_on` so each lands behind the previous one's contract. WS1 is the ready entry; WS6 is gated on `d251`.
1. **WS1 â€” diff context-assembler + base reviewer** (8e6f-3ad2-1f24-4b8b / low-cloud-wreck) â€” ready now.
2. **WS2 â€” overlay catalog + finder/verify/coach prompts + code move-catalog** (8e78-d515-3a98-4eba / make-rant-buoy) â€” depends_on WS1.
3. **WS3 â€” `overlay_union` + `merge_findings` ops + `gates/code-review.yaml`** (8855-e5f9-05b4-4a04 / crow-knelt-fiber) â€” depends_on WS2.
4. **WS4 â€” `gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict` + source-separation** (98ff-d3b8-d2f7-4a86 / side-haiku-dew) â€” depends_on WS3.
5. **WS5 â€” secrets/security detectors (fail-closed)** (bba6-69b9-b9d4-4ac0 / corn-pub-oat) â€” depends_on WS3.
6. **WS6 â€” Rewire the Gerrit review-bot to the four-pass code review** (4119-1a4f-19ff-4cfa / virid-trench-stone) â€” depends_on WS4. d251's `review_bot` is shipped, so this is an OFFLINE adapter swap (no live env).
7. **WS6-b â€” Gerrit `LLM-Review` dogfood proof (live)** (0da5-162a-b41f-413c / van-withe-folly) â€” depends_on WS6; gated on the deployed AWS/Gerrit env.
8. **WS7 â€” Make Gerrit the only path into GitHub `main` (cutover) + contributor guide** (1bd2-1401-3a18-4031 / weedy-lath-abbey) â€” consumes d251/S6; live apply gated on GitHub admin access.

## Success Criteria
- The four-pass code-review capability consumes the shared `review_kernel` (no forked passes) and runs as rebar's `LLM-Review` voter in Gerrit, off by default and source-isolated (inert and unmistakably non-applicable when disabled).
- rebar's own changes are reviewed and gated by it end-to-end through the Gerrit gate (dogfood), with Gerrit the ONLY path into GitHub `main` (WS7 cutover) and a `CONTRIBUTING.md` documenting the contributor flow.

## Specifics pinned (plan-review remediation 2026-06)
- **Back-out for the destructive single-pass retirement (WS4):** the capability is OFF by default, so the new four-pass gate is INERT until enabled; the preserved public surface (re-export shim) keeps existing callers working. **Correction (Round-11):** because the single-pass code is DELETED, reverting the flag default does NOT restore single-pass behavior â€” it makes the gate inert, with `review_code()` returning a valid EMPTY `review_result` (+ a 'disabled' note), never an error/stub. So the back-out = default-off + shim returning the inert result; the ADR records this as a genuine one-way door (not a reversible behavior swap). This resolves the prior contradiction between calling WS4 a one-way door AND claiming the flag-revert restores prior behavior.
- **WS6 staged rollout (Round-11):** the Gerrit voter activates behind a per-project/staged opt-in before fleet-wide enforcement, so a BLOCK from an under-tested voter cannot gate all committers on day one. Detail on WS6 (4119).
- **WS6 staged rollout (Round-11):** the Gerrit voter activates behind a per-project/staged opt-in before fleet-wide enforcement, so a BLOCK from an under-tested voter cannot gate all committers on day one (detail on WS6/4119).
- **WS6 sequencing risk (it is last by necessity, not choice):** WS1-WS5 deliver a capability fully testable via the `rebar review-code` CLI + MCP `review_code` tool WITHOUT Gerrit, so the only WS6-new surface is the Gerrit REST/event wiring â€” de-riskable against a STUB Gerrit before `d251` lands. WS6 cannot move earlier because it depends on `d251` infra.

## Acceptance Criteria
The internal new-work dependencies are captured here as workstream ACs; each is a CHILD STORY (detailed ACs + file-impact + verify-commands live on the child). The epic is DONE when all children are closed.

### A. Capability (buildable now against the shipped kernel â€” no external blocker)
- [ ] **WS1** (8e6f-3ad2-1f24-4b8b / low-cloud-wreck) â€” diff context-assembler + base reviewer emitting enum-constrained `recommend_overlays` (standalone agent step).
- [ ] **WS2** (8e78-d515-3a98-4eba / make-rant-buoy) â€” overlay catalog (specialist reviewers with `applies_to` globs/content triggers) + a code verify preamble embedding `VERIFIER_RULES_SCAFFOLD` + a code move-catalog (`MOVE_REGISTRY_SCHEMA` instance) + a `threshold_for` resolver.
- [ ] **WS3** (8855-e5f9-05b4-4a04 / crow-knelt-fiber) â€” `overlay_union` (glob U recommend âˆ’ already-run, ONE-HOP, cap N=3) + `merge_findings` + the thin `gates/code-review.yaml` (two-round escalation â†’ kernel Pass-2/3/4); offline FakeRunner test asserts escalation/cap/one-hop and that escalation can never change the verdict.
- [ ] **WS4** (98ff-d3b8-d2f7-4a86 / side-haiku-dew) â€” `gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict` returning the typed `code_review_verdict` + sidecar; OFF by default + SOURCE-SEPARATED; public surface preserved via re-export/shim; single-pass route retired (ADR).
- [ ] **WS5** (bba6-69b9-b9d4-4ac0 / corn-pub-oat) â€” secrets/security detectors (wrap gitleaks + semgrep behind the grounding-oracle envelope) feeding the hard auto-block; FAIL-CLOSED with a RED-fixture test (unavailable/errored detector BLOCKS or flags a coverage gap, never silently passes).

### B. Gerrit integration + cutover (d251 shipped the receiver; the LIVE steps are gated on the deployed env / GitHub admin)
- [ ] **WS6** (4119-1a4f-19ff-4cfa / virid-trench-stone) â€” REWIRE d251's shipped `review_bot.adapter.code_review_decision` over `produce_code_review_verdict` (four-pass), with a fail-closed vote mapping + a coverage-gap-vs-finding message distinction + a force-enable override; OFFLINE-verifiable (no live env).
- [ ] **WS6-b** (0da5-162a-b41f-413c / van-withe-folly) â€” LIVE dogfood: at least one real rebar change reviewed + gated end-to-end on the deployed AWS/Gerrit env (gated on env access).
- [ ] **WS7** (1bd2-1401-3a18-4031 / weedy-lath-abbey) â€” make Gerrit the ONLY path into GitHub `main` (consume d251/S6 mirror-lock; remove the legacy protection) + a repo-root `CONTRIBUTING.md` contributor guide, with a REQUIRED S6-based rollback/emergency-restore procedure (fast un-lock + restore of the `main-protection` snapshot 18048287) so a Gerrit outage cannot permanently freeze `main` (the live apply + rollback drill gated on GitHub admin access).
compiled_state.title: Agentic code-review capability (off-by-default, source-separated); dogfood on rebar via Gerrit LLM-Review gate
